steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 argues that genre classification is unhelpful — genres are "ambiguous, subjective, and simplistic." The question asks how Text 1's author would respond to Costa's claim that genre classification benefits listeners.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: Raises concerns about listener preferences — a topic Text 1 does not discuss. ✗
  • Choice B: Argues that improving a flawed framework does not make it useful — directly consistent with Text 1's skepticism that genre classification is a poor framework to begin with. ✓
  • Choice C: Treats genre classification as fundamentally sound, contradicting Text 1. ✗
  • Choice D: Also treats genre classification as valuable, contradicting Text 1. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
